title: Singapore design value: role value, user-centricity, advocacy effort, and compensation fairness
summary: Among Singapore designers: 53.3% feel their role is valued (ratings 5-7 of 169 valid responses). 42.9% say their organisation genuinely prioritises user needs (of 168 valid responses). 70.4% invest significant extra effort educating cross-functional teams about design (of 169 valid responses). 49.1% feel fairly compensated for their role and level, while 50.9% do not (of 169 valid responses). These figures are Singapore-only and are not comparable to the regional figures reported elsewhere in the 2024 report.
methodology: Self-reported perception of design value, compensation fairness, user-centricity, and advocacy efforts in an anonymous online survey, filtered to Singapore respondents (Country == 'Singapore'). Each question is a 7-point Likert scale bucketed as agree (ratings 5-7) versus disagree or neutral (ratings 1-4); blank and non-numeric responses are excluded from both the bucket counts and the valid-response base.
